title: Home Cooks Bring Back Old British Foods
summary: Some home cooks are making old British dishes that many people have forgotten. Annie Mae Herring is a food creator who tries recipes like carrageen pudding, which is a dessert made with seaweed. She also makes other foods like Bedfordshire clanger, a pasty that is sweet on one side and savory on the other. People watch her videos and remember eating these dishes when they were kids. They share stories about school dinners and family meals. Another cook, Shannon McCarthy, also explores old recipes from different parts of the UK. This helps keep these foods alive and brings back happy memories for many.

background_read: This article is about home cooks who are bringing back old British dishes that many people have forgotten. These dishes include desserts and pastries that were popular in the past, especially in schools or with families. By making and sharing videos of these foods, the cooks help people remember happy times and keep the recipes from being lost forever. It shows how food can connect people to their history and create fun memories.
Article_Structure: WHO: Annie Mae Herring and Shannon McCarthy, home cooks who make old British dishes.
WHAT: They are reviving forgotten foods like carrageen pudding and Bedfordshire clanger by cooking and sharing them online.
WHERE: In the United Kingdom, with recipes from different regions.
WHY: To preserve culinary history and evoke nostalgia among viewers who remember these dishes from childhood.

perspectives.description: The cooks are helping save old recipes from being lost, which is important for keeping British food traditions alive.
